message:
feat(rchd,rch): integrate disk-pressure policy into daemon lifecycle, worker selection, status reporting, and hook shell safety

Wire the new disk_pressure module into the full rchd daemon stack and fix a
shell-escaping correctness issue in the rch hook. This commit implements the
daemon-side integration points for disk-pressure-aware scheduling and status
reporting (bd-vvmd.4.2, bd-vvmd.4.5).

Why these changes exist together:
Disk-pressure monitoring is only useful if the pressure assessments actually
influence worker selection, appear in status/diagnostic surfaces, and are
stored on worker state. These changes form the integration layer that connects
the pressure policy engine to every consumer: the scheduler preflight gate,
the daemon status API, the worker capabilities endpoint, and the daemon
startup sequence. The hook.rs changes fix a shell-safety bug discovered during
the same work.

Detailed breakdown by file:

rchd/src/main.rs:
- Register the `disk_pressure` module in the crate module tree.
- Import DiskPressureMonitor and DiskPressurePolicyConfig.
- Start the disk-pressure background monitor during daemon initialization,
  after the telemetry poller, so pressure assessments begin flowing before
  the first build request arrives.

rchd/src/workers.rs:
- Add `pressure_assessment: RwLock<PressureAssessment>` field to WorkerState,
  initialized to the default TelemetryGap/Low-confidence state.
- Add `set_pressure_assessment()` and `pressure_assessment()` async accessor
  methods so the monitor can write and the scheduler/status can read.
- Extend WorkerCapabilitiesInfo struct with `pressure_assessment` field so
  the `rch workers capabilities` command exposes pressure metadata alongside
  existing capability data.
- Wire pressure_assessment into get_workers_capabilities() response builder.

rchd/src/selection.rs:
- Import PressureState for pattern matching in the preflight gate.
- Add a `hard_preflight_block` flag to distinguish pressure-Critical exclusions
  (which must not participate in fail-open fallback) from soft preflight failures
  (which still serve as fallback candidates).
- Add pressure policy evaluation after existing preflight checks: Critical workers
  are hard-excluded and increment filtered_by_hard_preflight; Warning workers log
  a diagnostic but remain eligible; TelemetryGap workers remain eligible (fail-open
  semantics); Healthy workers pass silently.
- Two new integration tests:
  - test_pressure_preflight_rejects_critical_worker_only_pool: verifies that a
    pool containing only a Critical-pressure worker returns no selection with
    AllWorkersFailedPreflight reason.
  - test_pressure_preflight_allows_telemetry_gap_fail_open: verifies that a
    TelemetryGap worker remains selectable, preserving fail-open behavior when
    telemetry is unavailable.

rchd/src/api.rs:
- Extend WorkerStatusInfo with 11 pressure-related fields (pressure_state,
  pressure_confidence, pressure_reason_code, pressure_policy_rule,
  pressure_disk_free_gb, pressure_disk_total_gb, pressure_disk_free_ratio,
  pressure_disk_io_util_pct, pressure_memory_pressure,
  pressure_telemetry_age_secs, pressure_telemetry_fresh) so the `rch status`
  command provides full pressure observability per worker.
- Wire pressure assessment reading in handle_status() for each worker.
- Add pressure-aware issue generation: Critical pressure emits an error-severity
  issue with remediation hint; TelemetryGap emits a warning-severity issue.
- Update existing serialization test to include pressure fields.
- Add test_handle_status_includes_pressure_metadata: validates that setting a
  Warning-level pressure assessment on a worker produces correct JSON fields.
- Add test_handle_status_emits_critical_pressure_issue: validates that Critical
  pressure generates an error-severity issue in the status response.
- Update WorkerCapabilitiesInfo serialization test for new pressure_assessment field.

rch/src/hook.rs:
- Extract `build_remote_shell_command()` helper function that properly wraps a
  remote command string with `sh -lc` and shell_escape quoting. Previously,
  `run_worker_ssh_command()` passed `sh`, `-lc`, and the raw command as three
  separate arguments to SSH, which could cause the remote shell to split the
  command on whitespace instead of treating it as a single script. The new
  helper passes the entire `sh -lc '<escaped>'` as one SSH argument string.
- Extract `build_remote_dependency_preflight_command()` from inline code in
  `verify_remote_dependency_manifests()`. The extracted function also fixes a
  bug where consecutive `if/fi` check blocks were joined with space instead
  of semicolon, which could cause syntax errors on some shells when multiple
  sync roots are present.
- Three new unit tests:
  - test_build_remote_dependency_preflight_command_empty_roots: verifies None
    return for empty input.
  - test_build_remote_dependency_preflight_command_separates_checks: verifies
    that multiple check blocks are separated by semicolons, not spaces.
  - test_build_remote_shell_command_wraps_and_escapes_script: verifies that
    the wrapper quotes the script as a single argument to sh -lc.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
